reasoning: Pedro Sánchez remains the Prime Minister of Spain and shows no signs of resigning or calling early elections. Searches confirm that as of August 2, 2025, he has just begun his summer family vacation in Lanzarote. The Spanish system requires a "constructive motion of no confidence" to remove a Prime Minister, meaning the opposition must agree on an alternative candidate with an absolute majority (176 seats). The main opposition party (PP) explicitly ruled out filing such a motion in June 2025 because they do not have the votes and see it as counterproductive. Moreover, Sánchez has publicly stated his goal of completing the legislature until 2027. Given that snap elections require 54 days, an election called in the fall would barely resolve before the end of the year, maximizing his chances of remaining in power at least through December 31, 2025. The base rate for survival in this short remaining timeframe is extremely high. The Metaculus community was at 95% in late July, and no destabilizing event has occurred since.
